title: M1 Abrams enters service
subtitle: Main battle tank family
fact: The M1 Abrams is a third-generation American main battle tank designed by Chrysler Defense and named for General Creighton Abrams.

title: Leopard 2 enters service
subtitle: German main battle tank
fact: The Leopard 2 is a third generation German main battle tank (MBT).

title: Stryker enters service
subtitle: Family of eight-wheeled armored fighting vehicles
fact: The Stryker is a family of eight-wheeled armored fighting vehicles derived from the Canadian LAV III, which in turn derived from the Swiss Mowag Piranha.

title: Merkava enters service
subtitle: Israeli main battle tank
fact: The Merkava is a series of main battle tanks used by the Israel Defense Forces (IDF) which are the backbone of the IDF's Armored Corps.

title: T-72 enters service
subtitle: Soviet main battle tank
fact: The T-72 is a family of Soviet main battle tanks that entered production in 1973.

title: T-34 enters service
subtitle: Soviet medium tank
fact: The T-34 is a Soviet medium tank from World War II.

title: M60 enters service
subtitle: American second generation main battle tank
fact: The M60 is an American second-generation main battle tank (MBT).

title: T-90 enters service
subtitle: Russian third-generation main battle tank
fact: The T-90 is a third-generation Russian main battle tank developed from, and designed to replace the T-72.

title: M113 enters service
subtitle: US armored personnel carrier
fact: The M113 is a fully tracked armored personnel carrier (APC) that was developed and produced by the FMC Corporation.

title: Buk missile system enters service
subtitle: Russian and Ukrainian surface-to-air missile system
fact: The Buk is a family of self-propelled, medium-range surface-to-air missile systems developed by the Soviet Union and its successor state, the Russian Federation, and designed to counter cruise missiles, smart bombs and rotary-wing aircraft, and unmanned aerial vehicles.

title: Panzer IV enters service
subtitle: German WWII medium tank
fact: The Panzerkampfwagen IV, commonly known as the Panzer IV, is a German medium tank developed in the late 1930s and used extensively during the Second World War.

title: Bell AH-1 SuperCobra enters service
subtitle: Series of twin-engine attack helicopters
fact: The Bell AH-1 SuperCobra is a twin-engined attack helicopter that was developed on behalf of, and primarily operated by, the United States Marine Corps (USMC).

title: M48 Patton enters service
subtitle: Main battle tank family
fact: The M48 Patton is an American first-generation main battle tank (MBT) introduced in February 1952, being designated as the 90mm Gun M48, armored, full-tracked, combat vehicle of the medium-gun tank class.

title: T-54/55 enters service
subtitle: Soviet main battle tank
fact: The T-54 and T-55 tanks are a series of Soviet medium tanks introduced in the years following the Second World War.

title: Panther enters service
subtitle: German medium tank
fact: The Panther tank, officially Panzerkampfwagen V Panther with ordnance inventory designation: Sd.Kfz. 171, is a German medium tank of World War II.

title: Challenger 2 enters service
subtitle: British main battle tank
fact: The FV4034 Challenger 2 is a third generation British main battle tank (MBT) in service with the armies of the United Kingdom, Oman, and Ukraine.

title: T-80 enters service
subtitle: Soviet main battle tank
fact: The T-80 is a main battle tank (MBT) that was designed and manufactured in the former Soviet Union and manufactured in Russia.

title: BM-21 Grad enters service
subtitle: Multiple launch rocket system family of Soviet origin
fact: The BM-21 "Grad" is a self-propelled 122 mm multiple rocket launcher designed in the Soviet Union.

title: K2 Black Panther enters service
subtitle: Main battle tank of the republic of korea armed forces
fact: K2 Black Panther is a South Korean fourth-generation main battle tank (MBT), designed by the Agency for Defense Development and manufactured by Hyundai Rotem.

title: Combat Vehicle 90 enters service
subtitle: Swedish infantry fighting vehicle
fact: The Combat Vehicle 90 (CV90) is a family of Swedish tracked armoured combat vehicles designed by the Swedish Defence Materiel Administration (FMV), Hägglund & Söner and Bofors during the mid-1980s to early 1990s, before entering service in Sweden in the mid-1990s.

title: Boxer enters service
subtitle: Multirole armoured fighting vehicle
fact: The Boxer is a family of armoured fighting vehicles designed by an international consortium to accomplish a number of operations through the use of installable mission modules.

title: 2K22 Tunguska enters service
subtitle: Self-propelled anti-aircraft weapon system
fact: The 2K22 Tunguska is a Soviet tracked self-propelled anti-aircraft gun armed with a surface-to-air gun and missile system.

title: M26 Pershing enters service
subtitle: American heavy battle tank at the end of World War Two
fact: The M26 Pershing is a heavy tank, later designated as a medium tank, formerly used by the United States Army during the early years of the Cold War.

title: M2 Bradley enters service
subtitle: US armored fighting vehicle
fact: The M2 Bradley, or Bradley IFV, is an American infantry fighting vehicle that is a member of the Bradley Fighting Vehicle family.

title: Centurion enters service
subtitle: British main battle tank
fact: The FV4007 (A41) Centurion was the primary main battle tank of the British Army during the post-World War II period.

title: T-62 enters service
subtitle: Soviet main battle tank
fact: The T-62 is a Soviet main battle tank that was first introduced in 1961.

title: LAV-25 enters service
subtitle: Reconnaissance vehicle
fact: The LAV-25 is a member of the LAV II family.

title: Panzer III enters service
subtitle: German medium tank
fact: The Panzerkampfwagen III, commonly known as the Panzer III, was a medium tank developed in the 1930s by Germany, and was used extensively in World War II.

title: T-64 enters service
subtitle: Soviet main battle tank
fact: The T-64 is a Soviet tank manufactured in Kharkiv, and designed by Alexander Morozov.

title: Joint Light Tactical Vehicle enters service
subtitle: Military Vehicle
fact: The Joint Light Tactical Vehicle (JLTV), known and marketed under Oshkosh development as the L-ATV, is a light utility/combat multi-role vehicle.

title: BTR-80 enters service
subtitle: Armored personnel carrier
fact: The BTR-80 is an 8×8 wheeled amphibious armoured personnel carrier (APC) designed in the Soviet Union.

title: Chieftain enters service
subtitle: Main battle tank family
fact: The FV4201 Chieftain was the primary main battle tank (MBT) of the United Kingdom from the 1960s into the 1990s.

title: 9K33 Osa enters service
subtitle: 1970S self-propelled surface-to-air missile system
fact: The 9K33 Osa is a highly mobile, low-altitude, short-range tactical surface-to-air missile system developed in the Soviet Union in the 1960s and fielded in 1972.

title: BMP-1 enters service
subtitle: Soviet infantry fighting vehicle family
fact: The BMP-1 is a Soviet amphibious tracked infantry fighting vehicle that has been in service from 1966 to the present.

title: BMP-2 enters service
subtitle: Soviet infantry fighting vehicle series
fact: The BMP-2 is an amphibious infantry fighting vehicle introduced in the 1980s in the Soviet Union, following on from the BMP-1 of the 1960s.

title: ZSU-23-4 Shilka enters service
subtitle: 1960S self-propelled quad 23 mm anti-aircraft gun system
fact: The ZSU-23-4 "Shilka" is a lightly armoured Soviet self-propelled, radar-guided anti-aircraft weapon system (SPAAG).

title: BMP-3 enters service
subtitle: Soviet infantry fighting vehicle family
fact: The BMP-3 is a Soviet and Russian infantry fighting vehicle, successor to the BMP-1 and BMP-2.

title: FV101 Scorpion enters service
subtitle: 1970S light tank model by Alvis
fact: The FV101 Scorpion is a British armoured reconnaissance vehicle and light tank.

title: Kliment Voroshilov tank enters service
subtitle: Family of Soviet heavy tanks
fact: The Kliment Voroshilov tanks are a series of Soviet heavy tanks named after the Soviet defence commissar and politician Kliment Voroshilov who operated with the Red Army during World War II.

title: Type 99 tank enters service
subtitle: Main battle tank
fact: The Type 99 or ZTZ-99 is a Chinese third generation main battle tank (MBT).

title: M1117 armored security vehicle enters service
subtitle: US armored security vehicle
fact: The M1117 armored security vehicle is an internal security vehicle based on the V-100 and V-150 Commando series of armored cars.

title: 2K12 Kub enters service
subtitle: 1960S self-propelled surface-to-air missile system
fact: The 2K12 "Kub" mobile surface-to-air missile system is a Soviet low to medium-level air defence system designed to protect ground forces from air attack.

title: Assault Amphibious Vehicle enters service
subtitle: Amphibious tracked armored personnel carrier series
fact: The Assault Amphibious Vehicle (AAV)—official designation AAVP-7A1 —is a fully tracked amphibious landing vehicle manufactured by BAE Systems Platforms & Services.

title: M47 Patton enters service
subtitle: American main battle tank
fact: The M47 Patton was an American medium tank, a development of the M46 Patton mounting an updated turret, and was in turn further developed as the M48 Patton.

title: IS-2 enters service
subtitle: Soviet heavy tank from World War II
fact: The IS-2 is a Soviet heavy tank, the second of the IS tank series named after Soviet leader Joseph Stalin.

title: Type 10 enters service
subtitle: Main battle tank
fact: The Type 10 is a Japanese fourth generation main battle tank produced by Mitsubishi Heavy Industries for the Japanese Ground Self Defense Force.

title: Challenger 3 enters service
subtitle: British main battle tank
fact: Challenger 3 (CR3) is a British fourth-generation main battle tank in development for the British Army.

title: AMX-30 enters service
subtitle: Main battle tank
fact: The AMX-30 is a French main battle tank designed by Ateliers de construction d'Issy-les-Moulineaux and first delivered to the French Army in August 1966.

title: BRDM-2 enters service
subtitle: Amphibious armored reconnaissance vehicle family
fact: The BRDM-2 is an amphibious armoured scout car designed and developed in the Soviet Union.

title: Commando enters service
subtitle: 1960S armored fighting vehicle family by Cadillac Gage
fact: The Cadillac Gage Commando, frequently denoted as the M706 in U.S. military service, is an American armored car designed to be amphibious.

title: Cromwell enters service
subtitle: British WWII cruiser tank
fact: The Cromwell tank, officially Tank, Cruiser, Mk VIII, Cromwell (A27M), was one of the series of cruiser tanks fielded by Britain in the Second World War.

title: BTR-60 enters service
subtitle: Soviet armored personnel carrier
fact: The BTR-60 is the first vehicle in a series of Soviet eight-wheeled armoured personnel carriers (APCs).

title: T-84 enters service
subtitle: Ukrainian main battle tank based on the Soviet T-80
fact: The T-84 is a Ukrainian main battle tank (MBT), based on the Soviet T-80 MBT introduced in 1976, specifically the diesel engine version: T-80UD.

title: Matilda II enters service
subtitle: WWII infantry tank
fact: The Infantry Tank Mark II, better known as the Matilda, is a British infantry tank of the Second World War.

title: Panzer II enters service
subtitle: German light tank
fact: The Panzer II is the common name used for a family of German tanks used in World War II.

title: Type 69 tank enters service
subtitle: Medium tank
fact: The Type 69 and Type 79 are Chinese first generation main battle tanks.

title: Altay enters service
subtitle: Turkish main battle tank
fact: Altay is a Turkish fourth generation main battle tank designed by Otokar, based on the South Korean K2 Black Panther.

title: A7V enters service
subtitle: German WW1 tank
fact: The Sturmpanzerwagen A7V was a heavy tank introduced by Germany in 1918 during World War I.

title: Apache AH1 enters service
subtitle: Attack helicopter series of the British Army
fact: The AgustaWestland Apache is a licence-built version of the Boeing AH-64D Apache Longbow attack helicopter for the British Army Air Corps.

title: PT-76 enters service
subtitle: Amphibious light tank family
fact: The PT-76 is a Soviet amphibious light tank that was introduced in the early 1950s and soon became the standard reconnaissance tank of the Soviet Army and the other Warsaw Pact armed forces.

title: Zulfiqar enters service
subtitle: Main battle tank
fact: Zulfiqar is an Iranian main battle tank, conceived by Brigadier General Mir-Younes Masoumzadeh, deputy ground force commander for research and self-sufficiency of the armed forces.

title: Patria AMV enters service
subtitle: Armored personnel carrier
fact: The Patria AMV is an 8×8 multi-role military vehicle produced by the Finnish defence industry company Patria.

title: M163 Vulcan Air Defense System enters service
subtitle: US self-propelled 20 mm anti-aircraft gun system
fact: The M163 Vulcan Air Defense System (VADS), officially Gun, Air Defense Artillery, Self-Propelled 20-mm, M163, is a self-propelled anti-aircraft gun (SPAAG) primarily used by the United States Army.

title: Panzer I enters service
subtitle: German light tank
fact: The Panzer I was a light tank produced by Nazi Germany in the 1930s.

title: T28 Super Heavy Tank enters service
subtitle: Prototype assault gun/tank destroyer
fact: The T28 super-heavy tank was an American super-heavy tank/assault gun designed for the United States Army during World War II.

title: Karl-Gerät enters service
subtitle: 1940S self-propelled siege mortar of German origin
fact: Karl-Gerät (040/041), also known as Mörser Karl, was a World War II German self-propelled siege mortar (Mörser) designed and built by Rheinmetall.

title: T-26 enters service
subtitle: Soviet light infantry tank
fact: The T-26 tank was a Soviet light tank used during many conflicts of the Interwar period and in World War II.

title: Sherman Firefly enters service
subtitle: Tank created by Britain during World War II
fact: The Sherman Firefly was a medium tank used by the United Kingdom and some armoured formations of other Allies in the Second World War.

title: M46 Patton enters service
subtitle: Type of medium tank
fact: The M46 Patton is an American medium tank designed to replace the M26 Pershing and M4 Sherman.

title: Challenger 1 enters service
subtitle: Main battle tank
fact: The FV4030/4 Challenger 1 is a British main battle tank (MBT) used by the British Army from 1983 to 2001, when it was superseded by the Challenger 2.

title: Bushmaster Protected Mobility Vehicle enters service
subtitle: Armored vehicle
fact: The Bushmaster Protected Mobility Vehicle or Infantry Mobility Vehicle is an Australian-built four-wheel drive armoured vehicle.

title: M-84 enters service
subtitle: Main battle tank
fact: The M-84 is a Yugoslav main battle tank based on the Soviet T-72.

title: K21 enters service
subtitle: Infantry fighting vehicle
fact: The K21 is a South Korean infantry fighting vehicle.

title: Char 2C enters service
subtitle: French super-heavy tank developed during World War I
fact: The Char 2C, also known as the FCM 2C, was a French post WWI heavy tank landship, later considered a super-heavy tank.

title: ZSU-57-2 enters service
subtitle: Self-propelled anti-aircraft weapon
fact: The ZSU-57-2 Ob'yekt 500 is a Soviet self-propelled anti-aircraft gun (SPAAG), armed with two 57 mm autocannons.

title: Universal Carrier enters service
subtitle: Armored personnel carrier
fact: The Universal Carrier, a development of the earlier Bren Gun Carrier from its light machine gun armament, was one of a family of light armoured tracked vehicles built by Vickers-Armstrongs and other companies.

title: Véhicule de l'Avant Blindé enters service
subtitle: Armored personnel carrier family
fact: The Véhicule de l'Avant Blindé is a French armoured personnel carrier and support vehicle designed and manufactured by Renault Trucks Defense.

title: Marder enters service
subtitle: 1970S infantry fighting vehicle family
fact: The Schützenpanzer Marder 1 is a tracked German infantry fighting vehicle designed for use with the West German Panzergrenadiere units, mechanized infantry specialized for IFV combat.

title: T-10 enters service
subtitle: Soviet heavy tank family
fact: The T-10 was a Soviet heavy tank of the Cold War, the final development of the IS tank series.

title: T-44 enters service
subtitle: Medium tank
fact: The T-44 was a medium tank developed and produced near the end of World War II by the Soviet Union.

title: Al-Khalid tank enters service
subtitle: Main battle tank
fact: The Al-Khalid/VT-1A is a main battle tank family developed jointly by Norinco of China and Heavy Industries Taxila of Pakistan.

title: Ariete enters service
subtitle: Battle tank of the Italian Army
fact: The C1 Ariete is a 3rd generation main battle tank of the Italian Army, developed by Consorzio Iveco Oto Melara (CIO), a consortium formed by Iveco and OTO Melara.

title: L3/35 enters service
subtitle: Type of Tankette
fact: The L3/35, also known as the Carro Veloce CV-35, was an Italian tankette that saw combat before and during World War II.

title: M42 Duster enters service
subtitle: 1950S self-propelled twin 40 mm anti-aircraft gun system
fact: The M42 40 mm self-propelled anti-aircraft gun, or Duster, is an American armored light air-defense gun built for the United States Army from 1952 until December 1960, in service until 1988.

title: Comet enters service
subtitle: Medium (cruiser) tank
fact: The Comet tank or Tank, Cruiser, Comet I (A34) was a British cruiser tank that first saw use near the end of the Second World War, during the Western Allied invasion of Germany.

title: Namer enters service
subtitle: Armored Personnel Carrier
fact: Namer is an Israeli armoured personnel carrier based on a Merkava Mark IV tank chassis.

title: MaxxPro enters service
subtitle: Infantry mobility vehicle
fact: The International M1224 MaxxPro MRAP is an armored fighting vehicle designed by American company Navistar International's subsidiary, Navistar Defense, which both designed and manufactured the vehicle's armour.

title: ASCOD enters service
subtitle: Armored fighting vehicle
fact: The ASCOD armoured fighting vehicle family is the product of a cooperation agreement between Austrian Steyr-Daimler-Puch AG and Spanish General Dynamics Santa Bárbara Sistemas.

title: M103 enters service
subtitle: Heavy tank
fact: The M103 heavy tank was a heavy tank that served in the United States Army and the United States Marine Corps during the Cold War.

title: Mark IV enters service
subtitle: British tank of World War I
fact: The Mark IV was a British tank of the First World War.

title: Mark V tank enters service
subtitle: British tank
fact: The British Mark V tank was an upgraded version of the Mark IV tank.

title: M50 Ontos enters service
subtitle: American anti-tank vehicle
fact: Ontos, officially the Rifle, Multiple 106 mm, Self-propelled, M50, was an American light armored tracked anti-tank vehicle developed in the 1950s.

title: M88 Recovery Vehicle enters service
subtitle: Armored recovery vehicle
fact: The M88 recovery vehicle is one of the largest armored recovery vehicles (ARV) in use by United States Armed Forces.

title: 152Mm SpGH DANA enters service
subtitle: Self-propelled artillery
fact: The DANA is a wheeled self-propelled artillery piece.

title: BTR-50 enters service
subtitle: 1950S armored personnel carrier of Soviet origin
fact: The BTR-50 is a Soviet tracked amphibious armoured personnel carrier (APC) based on the PT-76 light tank chassis.

title: ZBD-04 enters service
subtitle: Infantry fighting vehicle
fact: The ZBD-04 or Type 04 is a Chinese infantry fighting vehicle.

title: BTR-70 enters service
subtitle: Armored personnel carrier
fact: The BTR-70 is an eight-wheeled armored personnel carrier originally developed by the Soviet Union during the late 1960s under the manufacturing code GAZ-4905.

title: Ratel IFV enters service
subtitle: Infantry fighting vehicle
fact: The Ratel is a South African infantry fighting vehicle.

title: Saint-Chamond enters service
subtitle: Medium tank
fact: The Saint-Chamond was the second French tank to enter service during the First World War, with 400 manufactured from April 1917 to July 1918.

title: WZ551 enters service
subtitle: Type of infantry fighting vehicle / armored personnel carrier
fact: The WZ-551 is a Chinese wheeled infantry fighting vehicle family.

title: Type 74 enters service
subtitle: Main battle tank
fact: The Type 74 was a main battle tank (MBT) of the Japan Ground Self-Defense Force (JGSDF).

title: Type 88 tank enters service
subtitle: Chinese second generation main battle tank
fact: The Type 80 and the Type 88 are a family of Chinese second-generation main battle tanks (MBTs).

title: Pokpung-ho enters service
subtitle: Ropiendo el aire
fact: The P'okp'ung-ho, officially the Chonma-215 and Chonma-216 are North Korean main battle tanks (MBT) developed in the 1990s.

title: Nora B-52 enters service
subtitle: Self-propelled artillery
fact: The Nora B-52 is a Serbian 155 mm self-propelled howitzer designed by the Military Technical Institute and manufactured by Yugoimport SDPR.

title: Leclerc enters service
subtitle: Main battle tank
fact: The Leclerc is a third-generation French main battle tank developed and manufactured by KNDS France.

title: M22 Locust enters service
subtitle: Airborne forces
fact: The M22 Locust, officially Light Tank (Airborne), M22, was an American-designed airborne light tank which was produced during World War II.

title: K30 Biho enters service
subtitle: Self-propelled anti-aircraft weapon
fact: The South Korean K30 Biho twin 30 mm self-propelled anti-aircraft weapon was developed to meet the operational requirements of the Republic of Korea Armed Forces for a highly mobile short range air defense system suited to the operational and terrain conditions of the Korean peninsula.

title: 2K11 Krug enters service
subtitle: 1960S mobile surface-to-air missile system
fact: The 2K11 Krug is a Soviet and now Russian medium-range, medium-to-high altitude surface-to-air missile (SAM) system.

title: Leichter Panzerspähwagen enters service
subtitle: German scout car family
fact: The Leichter Panzerspähwagen was a series of light four-wheel drive armoured cars produced by Nazi Germany from 1935 to 1944.

title: Schwerer Panzerspähwagen enters service
subtitle: German armored car family
fact: The Schwerer Panzerspähwagen, is a series of six- and eight-wheeled armoured cars that were used by Germany during the Second World War.

title: TKS enters service
subtitle: Type of Tankette
fact: The TK (TK-3) and TKS were Polish tankettes developed during the 1930s and used in the Second World War.

title: Rooikat enters service
subtitle: Type of armored fighting vehicle
fact: The Rooikat is a South African armoured reconnaissance vehicle equipped with a stabilised 76 mm (3.0 in) high velocity gun for organic anti-tank and fire support purposes.

title: Daimler Dingo enters service
subtitle: British scout car family
fact: The Daimler scout car, known in service as the Daimler Dingo, is a British light, fast four-wheel drive reconnaissance vehicle also used for liaison during the Second World War.

title: FV510 Warrior enters service
subtitle: British infantry fighting vehicle family
fact: The FV510 Warrior tracked vehicle family is a series of British armoured vehicles, originally developed to replace FV430 series armoured vehicles.

title: BMD-1 enters service
subtitle: Infantry fighting vehicle
fact: The BMD-1 is a Soviet airborne amphibious tracked infantry fighting vehicle (IFV), which was introduced in 1969 and first seen by the West in 1970.

title: BTR-40 enters service
subtitle: 1950S armored personnel carrier family of Soviet origin
fact: The BTR-40 is a Soviet open-topped, wheeled armoured personnel carrier and reconnaissance vehicle.

title: BT-7 enters service
subtitle: Soviet light tank
fact: The BT-7 was the last of the BT series of Soviet cavalry tanks that were produced in large numbers between 1935 and 1940.

title: Tigr enters service
subtitle: Russian infantry vehicle
fact: The Tigr is a Russian 4×4 multipurpose all-terrain infantry mobility vehicle manufactured by Military Industrial Company, first delivered to the Russian Army in 2006.

title: PL-01 enters service
subtitle: Polish main battle tank
fact: The PL-01 was a Polish light tank concept created by OBRUM with support from BAE Systems, based on the Swedish CV90120-T light tank.

title: ARL 44 enters service
subtitle: French heavy tank
fact: The ARL 44 was a French heavy tank and tank destroyer, the development of which started just before the end of the Second World War.

title: Ram enters service
subtitle: Medium cruiser tank
fact: The Ram was a cruiser tank designed and built by Canada in the Second World War, based on the U.S.

title: T-28 enters service
subtitle: Soviet medium tank
fact: The T-28 was a Soviet multi-turreted medium tank.

title: Freccia enters service
subtitle: Type of wheeled infantry fighting vehicle
fact: The VBM – Freccia is a family of 8×8 armoured vehicles that was designed and manufactured for the Italian Army by Iveco Defence Vehicles for the hull, the engine and the suspension, and OTO Melara for the armament.

title: Schneider CA1 enters service
subtitle: Type of Tank
fact: The Schneider CA 1 was the first French tank, developed during the First World War.

title: Tortoise heavy assault tank enters service
subtitle: British tank concept
fact: The Tortoise heavy assault tank (A39) was a British heavy assault gun tank design developed during the Second World War, but never put into mass production.

title: Vickers MBT enters service
subtitle: Main battle tank family
fact: The Vickers MBT is a series of main battle tanks (MBTs) developed as a private venture by British company Vickers-Armstrongs for export.

title: DD tank enters service
subtitle: British WWII amphibious swimming tank
fact: DD or duplex drive tanks, nicknamed "Donald Duck tanks", were a type of amphibious swimming tank developed by the British during the Second World War.

title: Carden Loyd tankette enters service
subtitle: Type of tankette
fact: The Carden Loyd tankettes were a series of British tankettes of the period between the World Wars, the most successful of which was the Mark VI, the only version built in significant numbers.

title: T-70 enters service
subtitle: Soviet light tank
fact: The T-70 is a light tank used by the Red Army during World War II, replacing both the T-60 scout tank for reconnaissance and the T-50 light infantry tank for infantry support.

title: OT-64 SKOT enters service
subtitle: 1960S armored personnel carrier family
fact: The OT-64 SKOT is an amphibious armored personnel carrier (8×8), developed jointly by the Polish People's Republic (PRL) and Czechoslovakia (ČSSR) well into the 1960s.

title: M6 heavy tank enters service
subtitle: Type of heavy tank
fact: The Heavy Tank M6 was an American heavy tank designed during World War II.

title: Sentinel AC enters service
subtitle: Cruiser tank
fact: The AC1 Sentinel was a cruiser tank designed in Australia in World War II in response to the war in Europe, and to the threat of Japan expanding the war to the Pacific or even a feared Japanese invasion of Australia.

title: TR-85 enters service
subtitle: Main battle tank
fact: The TR-85 is a main battle tank designed for the armed forces of Romania.

title: 7TP enters service
subtitle: Polish light tank model
fact: The 7TP was a Polish light tank of the Second World War.

title: Mitsubishi Type 89 IFV enters service
subtitle: Infantry fighting vehicle
fact: The Mitsubishi Type 89 IFV (89 FV ) is a Japanese infantry fighting vehicle that entered service with the Japan Ground Self-Defense Force in 1989.
image: 普戦チームの攻撃_011_R_富士総合火力演習・そうかえん_46.jpg

title: Otokar Cobra enters service
subtitle: Armored fighting vehicle
fact: The Cobra is a armoured tactical vehicle developed by Turkish firm Otokar.

title: SU-85 enters service
subtitle: Soviet tank destroyer
fact: The SU-85 was a Soviet self-propelled gun used during World War II, based on the chassis of the T-34 medium tank.

title: Kangaroo enters service
subtitle: Armored personnel carrier
fact: A Kangaroo was a Canadian armoured personnel carrier (APC) during the Second World War which was created by converting a tank chassis.

title: Type 61 enters service
subtitle: Main battle tank
fact: The Type 61 tank is a main battle tank developed and used by the Japan Ground Self-Defense Force (JGSDF), built by Mitsubishi Heavy Industries.

title: Otokar Cobra II enters service
subtitle: Infantry mobility vehicle with MRAP capabilities
fact: The Cobra II, a further development of the earlier Cobra I model, is an armoured tactical vehicle designed and produced by Turkish company Otokar.

title: Vickers 6-Ton enters service
subtitle: British light tank
fact: The Vickers 6-ton tank or Vickers Mark E, also known as the "Six-tonner", was a British light tank designed in 1928 in a private project at Vickers.

title: Kanonenjagdpanzer enters service
subtitle: German tank destroyer
fact: The Kanonenjagdpanzer (KanJPz) was a West German Cold War tank destroyer.

title: Type 94 tankette enters service
subtitle: Type of Tankette
fact: The Type 94 tankette was a tankette used by the Imperial Japanese Army in the Second Sino-Japanese War, at Nomonhan against the Soviet Union, and in World War II.

title: Tsar Tank enters service
subtitle: Russian tank with improvided armor
fact: The Tsar Tank, also known as the Netopyr' or Lebedenko Tank, was a Russian armoured vehicle developed by Nikolai Lebedenko, Nikolay Yegorovich Zhukovsky, Boris Stechkin, and Alexander Mikulin from 1914 onwards.

title: OF-40 enters service
subtitle: Italian main battle tank
fact: The OF-40 is an Italian main battle tank developed as a joint venture between OTO Melara and Fiat, and intended primarily for export sales.

title: T-50 enters service
subtitle: Soviet light tank
fact: The T-50 was a light infantry tank built by the Soviet Union at the beginning of World War II.

title: Daimler armoured car enters service
subtitle: British armoured car family
fact: The Daimler armoured car was a successful British armoured car design of the Second World War that continued in service into the 1950s.

title: Medium Mark A Whippet enters service
subtitle: Medium tank
fact: The Medium Mark A Whippet was a medium tank employed by the British in World War I.

title: Six Ton Tank M1917 enters service
subtitle: Type of light tank
fact: The M1917 was the United States' first mass-produced tank, entering production shortly before the end of World War I.

title: Panzer 61 enters service
subtitle: Medium tank
fact: The Panzer 61 was a Swiss Cold War era medium tank later reclassified as a second-generation main battle tank.

title: BT-42 enters service
subtitle: Finnish WW2 assault gun
fact: The BT-42 was a Finnish assault gun, constructed during the Continuation War.

title: Bandkanon 1 enters service
subtitle: Self-propelled gun
fact: 15,5 cm bandkanon 1, meaning "15.5 cm (6.1 in) tracked cannon 1", was a Swedish self-propelled artillery vehicle in use with the Swedish Army from 1967 to 2003, developed by Aktiebolaget Bofors.

title: BTR-D enters service
subtitle: Type of airborne amphibious multi-purpose tracked armored personnel carrier
fact: The BTR-D is a Soviet airborne multi-purpose tracked armoured personnel carrier.

title: Covenanter tank enters service
subtitle: Type of cruiser tank
fact: The Cruiser tank Mk V or A13 Mk III Covenanter was a British cruiser tank of the Second World War.

title: M59 enters service
subtitle: 1950S armored personnel carrier of American origin
fact: The M59 was an American armored personnel carrier that entered service in the spring of 1954 replacing the M75.

title: T-18 tank enters service
subtitle: Light tank
fact: The T-18 light tank was the first Soviet-designed tank.

title: BMD-2 enters service
subtitle: Soviet Infantry Fighting Vehicle
fact: The BMD-2 is a Soviet airborne infantry fighting vehicle, introduced in 1985.

title: Type 89 AFV enters service
subtitle: Armored personnel carrier
fact: The Norinco Type 89 tracked armoured fighting vehicle is a Chinese armoured personnel carrier.

title: Sisu GTP enters service
subtitle: Type of MRAP APC
fact: The Sisu GTP is a Finnish-made four-wheeled, modular mine-resistant ambush protected infantry mobility vehicle (IMV) designed and built by Sisu Auto, although the chassis and the powertrain is based on the Mercedes-Benz Unimog.

title: Eland Mk7 enters service
subtitle: South African light armoured car
fact: The Eland is an air-portable light armoured car based on the Panhard AML.

title: 40M Turán I enters service
subtitle: Hungarian medium tank
fact: The Turán tanks were a series of Hungarian medium tanks of World War II.

title: Kamov Ka-52 Alligator enters service
subtitle: Attack helicopter series by Kamov
fact: The Kamov Ka-50 "Black Shark", NATO reporting name Hokum A, is a Soviet/Russian single-seat attack helicopter with the distinctive coaxial rotor system of the Kamov design bureau.

title: MIM-72 Chaparral enters service
subtitle: Surface-to-air missile system
fact: The M48 Chaparral is an American-made self-propelled surface-to-air missile based on the M113 family of vehicles.

title: M2 enters service
subtitle: 1930S United States Army tank
fact: The M2 medium tank, officially Medium Tank, M2, was a United States Army medium tank that was first produced in 1939 by the Rock Island Arsenal, just prior to the commencement of the Second World War in Europe.

title: Nurol Ejder enters service
subtitle: Type of armored combat vehicle
fact: The Nurol Ejder is a family of armoured vehicles produced by Turkish company Nurol Makina.

title: RG-33 enters service
subtitle: Light armored combat vehicle
fact: The RG-33 is a mine-resistant light armored vehicle initially designed by BAE Systems Land Systems South Africa, a South African subsidiary of BAE Systems.

title: BMD-3 enters service
subtitle: Infantry vehicle
fact: The BMD-3 is a light infantry fighting vehicle originating in the Soviet Union that is fully amphibious and air-droppable with crew inside.

title: Vijayanta enters service
subtitle: Military tank from India
fact: The Vijayanta was a main battle tank built in India based on a licensed design of the Vickers Mk.1.

title: Type 3 Chi-Nu enters service
subtitle: Medium tank
fact: Type 3 medium tank Chi-Nu was a medium tank of the Imperial Japanese Army in World War II.

title: Grille enters service
subtitle: Self-propelled howitzer
fact: The 15 cm sIG 33 (Sf) auf Panzerkampfwagen 38(t), also known as Grille was a series of self-propelled artillery vehicles used by Nazi Germany during World War II.

title: ASU-57 enters service
subtitle: 1950S self-propelled 57 mm gun
fact: The ASU-57 was a small, lightly constructed Soviet assault gun specifically designed for use by Soviet airborne divisions.

title: Humber armored car enters service
subtitle: 1940S British armored car family
fact: The Humber armoured car was one of the most widely produced British armoured cars of the Second World War.

title: Armored Vehicle General Purpose enters service
subtitle: Armored fighting vehicle
fact: The AVGP, later known as the LAV I, is a series of three amphibious armoured fighting vehicles ordered by the Canadian military in the 1970s.

title: Matilda I enters service
subtitle: British infantry tank
fact: The Tank, Infantry, Mk I, Matilda I (A11) is a British infantry tank of the Second World War.

title: Lazar 3 enters service
subtitle: Type of armored personnel carrier
fact: The Lazar 3 is the latest version of the Lazar family of armoured vehicles, designed for various applications and missions.

title: T-60 enters service
subtitle: Soviet light tank
fact: The T-60 scout tank was a light tank produced by the Soviet Union from 1941 to 1942.

title: Tosan enters service
subtitle: Type of light tank
fact: The Tosan is an Iranian light tank.

title: Marder I enters service
subtitle: Tank destroyer
fact: The Marder I "Marten" was a German World War II tank destroyer, armed with a 75 mm Pak-40 anti-tank gun.

title: CM-11 Brave Tiger enters service
subtitle: Type of main battle tank
fact: The CM-11 Brave Tiger is a Taiwanese main battle tank (MBT) that was developed by the American General Dynamics and the Republic of China Army Armored Vehicle Development Center.

title: M60 AVLB enters service
subtitle: Armored vehicle-launched bridge
fact: The M60 armored vehicle launched bridge (AVLB) is an armored vehicle based on the M60 Patton main battle tank's hull and used for the launching and retrieval of a 60-foot (18 m) scissors-type bridge.

title: Panzer 68 enters service
subtitle: Main battle tank
fact: The Panzer 68 was a Swiss main battle tank developed by the Eidgenoessische Konstruktionswerkstaette in Thun in the late 1960s, and was the main tank of the Swiss Army until the late 1990s.

title: Stridsvagn m/42 enters service
subtitle: Type of medium tank
fact: Stridsvagn m/42 was a Swedish medium tank in service in the World War II period.

title: M114 Command and Reconnaissance Carrier enters service
subtitle: 1960S tracked reconnaissance vehicle by Cadillac
fact: The M114 Command and Reconnaissance Carrier is a Vietnam War-era tracked armored fighting vehicle, used by the United States Army.

title: HS.30 enters service
subtitle: Infantry fighting vehicle model by Hispano-Suiza
fact: The Schützenpanzer Lang HS.30, officially designated as Schützenpanzer, lang, Typ 12-3 or abbreviated as SPz lg 12-3,, was a West German infantry fighting vehicle developed between 1956 and 1958.

title: Cruiser Mk I enters service
subtitle: Type of cruiser tank
fact: The Tank, Cruiser, Mk I (A9) was a British cruiser tank of the interwar period.

title: BA-64 enters service
subtitle: 1940S armored car family of Soviet origin
fact: The BA-64 was a Soviet four-wheeled armoured scout car.

title: Charioteer enters service
subtitle: 1950S British tank
fact: The Charioteer Tank, or FV4101 Tank, Medium Gun, Charioteer was a post–World War II British armoured fighting vehicle.

title: M16 Half-track enters service
subtitle: Self-propelled anti-aircraft weapon
fact: The M16 multiple gun motor carriage, also known as the M16 half-track, is an American self-propelled anti-aircraft weapon built during World War II.

title: Buffel enters service
subtitle: Type of MPAV
fact: The Buffel is an infantry mobility vehicle used by the South African Defence Force during the South African Border War.

title: PLZ-45 enters service
subtitle: Self-propelled artillery
fact: The PLZ-45 or Type 88 is a 155 mm self-propelled howitzer developed by Norinco.

title: 2A3 Kondensator 2P enters service
subtitle: Soviet self-propelled Howitzer
fact: The 2A3 Kondensator 2P was a Soviet 406 mm self-propelled howitzer.

title: Uran-9 enters service
subtitle: Russian tracked unmanned ground combat vehicle
fact: The Uran-9 is a tracked unmanned combat ground vehicle (UCGV) developed and produced by JSC 766 UPTK, and promoted and offered by Rosoboronexport for the international market.

title: Cruiser Mk IV enters service
subtitle: Type of cruiser tank
fact: The Cruiser Tank Mk IV was a British cruiser tank of the Second World War.

title: Rolls-Royce armoured car enters service
subtitle: Armored car
fact: The Rolls-Royce armoured car is a British armoured car developed in 1914 and used during the First World War, Irish Civil War, the inter-war period in Imperial Air Control in Transjordan, Palestine and Mesopotamia, and in the early stages of the Second World War in the Middle East and North Africa.

title: Strv L-60 enters service
subtitle: Swedish light tank
fact: The Landsverk L-60 was a Swedish tank developed in 1934.

title: Lion of Babylon enters service
subtitle: 80S era Iraqi project to produce tanks locally
fact: During the early stages of the Iran-Iraq War, Iraq under the rule of Saddam Hussein imported a number of T-72 tanks from the Soviet Union and Poland.

title: 1K17 Szhatie enters service
subtitle: Soviet laser tank
fact: The 1K17 Szhatie is a self-propelled laser vehicle of Soviet origin.

title: Semovente 75/18 enters service
subtitle: Self-propelled gun
fact: The Semovente da 75/18 was an Italian self-propelled gun of the Second World War.

title: Tulpar enters service
subtitle: Type of infantry fighting vehicle
fact: Tulpar is a Turkish heavy infantry fighting vehicle designed by the Sakarya-based automotive manufacturer Otokar.

title: Komatsu LAV enters service
subtitle: Type of Armoured Scout Car
fact: The Komatsu light armored vehicle (Japanese: 軽装甲機動車; is a Japanese military vehicle first produced in 2002.

title: Cruiser Mk II enters service
subtitle: Type of cruiser tank
fact: The Tank, Cruiser, Mk II (A10), was a cruiser tank developed alongside the A9 cruiser tank, and was intended to be a heavier, infantry tank version of that type.

title: FCM 36 enters service
subtitle: Light infantry tank that was designed for the French Army prior to World War II
fact: The FCM 36 or Char léger Modèle 1936 FCM, was a light infantry tank designed for the French Army prior to World War II.

title: Vickers Medium Mark II enters service
subtitle: British interwar medium tank
fact: The Vickers Medium Mark II was a British medium tank built by Vickers during the interwar period of the First and Second World Wars.

title: M75 enters service
subtitle: 1950S armored personnel carrier of American origin
fact: The M75 armored infantry vehicle is an American armored personnel carrier that was produced between December 1952 and February 1954, and saw service in the Korean War.

title: Char D1 enters service
subtitle: Type of light tank
fact: The Char D1 was an Interwar French light tank.

title: Dragoon AFV enters service
subtitle: Type of amphibious armoured vehicle
fact: The Dragoon 300 AFV was produced by Arrowpointe Corporation during the 1980s.

title: Panzerwerfer enters service
subtitle: German multiple rocket launcher
fact: The German Panzerwerfer refers to either of two different types of half-tracked multiple rocket launchers employed by Nazi Germany during the Second World War.

title: BA-10 enters service
subtitle: Soviet armored car
fact: The BA-10 was an armored car developed in the Soviet Union in 1938 and produced through 1941.

title: Type 97 ShinHoTo Chi-Ha enters service
subtitle: Medium tank
fact: The Type 97 Shinhōtō Chi-Ha was a Japanese medium tank used in World War II that was an upgrade to the original Type 97 Chi-Ha.

title: T-38 enters service
subtitle: Soviet amphibious tank
fact: The T-38 amphibious scout tank was a Soviet amphibious and reconnaissance light tank that saw service in World War II.

title: AMC 35 enters service
subtitle: Type of cavalry tank
fact: The AMC 35, also known under a manufacturer's designation Renault ACG-1, was a French medium cavalry tank of the later Interwar era that served in the Second World War.

title: Grizzly I cruiser enters service
subtitle: Type of medium tank
fact: The Tank, Cruiser, Grizzly, or Grizzly I, was a Canadian-built version of the M4A1 Sherman tank with modifications to stowage and pioneer tool location and accommodation for a Number 19 radio set.

title: ZSU-37 enters service
subtitle: Self-propelled anti-aircraft weapon
fact: ZSU-37 was a Soviet-made, light, self-propelled anti-aircraft gun (SPAAG), developed by the end of 1943 and produced at Works No. 40 in Mytishchi.

title: SMK tank enters service
subtitle: Heavy tank
fact: The SMK was an armored vehicle prototype developed by the Soviet Union prior to the Second World War.

title: AMR 35 enters service
subtitle: French light tank developed during the Interbellum
fact: The Automitrailleuse de Reconnaissance Renault Modèle 35 Type ZT was a French light tank developed during the Interbellum and used in the Second World War.

title: T-27 enters service
subtitle: Tankette
fact: The T-27 was a tankette produced in the 1930s by the Soviet Union.

title: Anoa enters service
subtitle: Indonesian 6x6 APC
fact: The Anoa is a 6×6 armoured personnel carrier developed by PT Pindad of Indonesia.

title: Type 86 infantry fighting vehicle enters service
subtitle: Infantry fighting vehicle
fact: The Type 86, also known as WZ501, is a Chinese variant of the Soviet BMP-1 infantry fighting vehicle (IFV).

title: Vickers A1E1 Independent enters service
subtitle: British tank type
fact: The Independent A1E1 is a multi-turreted tank that was designed by the British armaments manufacturer Vickers between the First and Second World Wars.

title: ELVO Leonidas-2 enters service
subtitle: Type of armoured personnel carrier
fact: The Leonidas-2 represented an effort made by the Greek vehicle manufacturer ELVO to produce an advanced armoured personnel carrier (APC) of its own.

title: MLI-84 enters service
subtitle: Type of infantry fighting vehicle
fact: The MLI-84 is a tracked Romanian infantry fighting vehicle currently in service with the Romanian Land Forces.

title: SU-100Y Self-Propelled Gun enters service
subtitle: Soviet prototype self-propelled gun
fact: The SU-100Y, initially designated T-100Y, was a Soviet prototype self-propelled gun, developed from the chassis of the prototype T-100 tank.

title: Puma enters service
subtitle: Wheeled armored personnel carrier family
fact: The Puma is an Italian light wheeled armoured fighting vehicle family, consisting of the Puma 6×6 and the Puma 4×4.

title: T92 Light Tank enters service
subtitle: Light tank
fact: The T92 Light Tank, or 76-mm Gun Tank, T92, was an American light tank developed in the 1950s by Aircraft Armaments.

title: Cruiser Mk III enters service
subtitle: British cruiser tank of the Second World War
fact: The Tank, Cruiser, Mk III, also known by its General Staff specification number A13 Mark I, was a British cruiser tank of the Second World War.

title: M39 Armored Utility Vehicle enters service
subtitle: Armored personnel carrier
fact: The M39 armored utility vehicle (T41) is an American armored vehicle designed during the Second World War, which saw service in that conflict and in the Korean War.

title: M19 Multiple Gun Motor Carriage enters service
subtitle: Type of self-propelled anti-aircraft weapon
fact: The M19 multiple gun motor carriage (MGMC) was a World War II United States Army self-propelled anti-aircraft weapon on the M24 light tank chassis.

title: Christie M1931 enters service
subtitle: Type of cruiser tank/medium tank
fact: The Christie M1931, known as the Combat Car, T1 in US Cavalry use and Medium/Light Tank, Convertible, T3 in Infantry branch, was a wheel-to-track tank designed by J.

title: Vickers Medium Mark I enters service
subtitle: Medium tank
fact: The Vickers Medium Mark I was a British tank of the Inter-war period built by Vickers from 1924.

title: M1 combat car enters service
subtitle: Type of light tank
fact: The M1 combat car, officially Light Tank, M1, was a light tank used by the United States Cavalry in the late 1930s and developed at the same time as the U.S.

title: Peresvet enters service
subtitle: Russian laser weapon system
fact: The Peresvet, named after Alexander Peresvet, is a Russian laser weapon for air defense and anti-satellite warfare.

title: AH-IV enters service
subtitle: Type of tankette
fact: The AH-IV was a Czechoslovak-designed export armored fighting vehicle, classed as either a tankette or light tank, used by Romania during World War II, but having also been acquired by neutral Sweden and Iran.

title: LAPV Enok enters service
subtitle: Armored car
fact: The LAPV Enok is a Light Armoured Patrol Vehicle of the Bundeswehr, mostly in use with the German Army.

title: Otokar Akrep enters service
subtitle: Type of infantry mobility vehicle
fact: Akrep ("Scorpion") is a Turkish infantry mobility vehicle family developed by Otokar Otobus Karoseri Sanayi AS.

title: Type 1 Ho-Ni I enters service
subtitle: Tank destroyer
fact: The Type 1 gun tank Ho-Ni I was a tank destroyer and self-propelled artillery developed by the Imperial Japanese Army for use during World War II in the Pacific theater.

title: M992 Field Artillery Ammunition Supply Vehicle enters service
subtitle: Military vehicle
fact: The M992 field artillery ammunition supply vehicle (FAASV) is built on the chassis of the M109 howitzer.

title: 43M Zrínyi enters service
subtitle: Assault gun
fact: The 43M Zrínyi II was a Hungarian assault gun of World War II based on the Turán medium tank chassis.

title: Type 1 Chi-He enters service
subtitle: Type of weapon
fact: The Type 1 medium tank Chi-He was an improved version of the Type 97 Chi-Ha medium tanks of the Imperial Japanese Army in World War II.

title: Sabra enters service
subtitle: Main battle tank
fact: The Sabra is a family of tank upgrade package developed by Israel Military Industries for export market.

title: T-37A enters service
subtitle: Soviet amphibious tank
fact: The T-37A was a Soviet amphibious light tank.

title: Stridsvagn 74 enters service
subtitle: Swedish light tank
fact: Stridsvagn 74 is a Swedish light tank in use with the Swedish Army from 1958 to 1984.

title: Wolf Armoured Vehicle enters service
subtitle: Israeli armored personnel carrier
fact: The Wolf Armoured Vehicle is an armoured personnel carrier, used mainly by the Israeli Defence Force.

title: SU-14 enters service
subtitle: Self-propelled gun
fact: The SU-14 was a prototype Soviet heavy self-propelled gun that started out as an open topped vehicle, with the armor being added later on when it was converted into a direct-fire assault gun in 1941.

title: Ramses II tank enters service
subtitle: Type of main battle tank
fact: The Ramses II tank is a heavily modernized T-55 main battle tank designed for and used by the Egyptian Armed Forces.

title: Jaguar 2 enters service
subtitle: Type of tank destroyer
fact: The Raketenjagdpanzer 4 Jaguar 2 was a West German tank destroyer equipped with anti-tank guided missiles.

title: BPM-97 enters service
subtitle: Armored personnel carrier
fact: The BPM-97 or Выстрел is the Russian military designation for the KAMAZ 43269 Vystrel 4×4 wheeled mine-resistant, ambush protected (MRAP) vehicle.

title: BA-20 enters service
subtitle: Soviet armored car
fact: The BA-20 was an armored car developed in the Soviet Union in 1934.

title: Type 95 SPAAA enters service
subtitle: Self-propelled anti-aircraft gun/missile system
fact: The PGZ-95 is a Chinese self-propelled anti-aircraft vehicle.

title: BTR-94 enters service
subtitle: Armored personnel carrier
fact: The BTR-94 is a Ukrainian amphibious armoured personnel carrier (Bronetransporter), a modification of the Soviet eight-wheeled BTR-80.

title: Fahd enters service
subtitle: Armored personnel carrier of Egyptian origin
fact: The Fahd is a 4x4 Egyptian armored personnel carrier (APC) produced by Kader Factory for Developed Industries, a subsidiary of Arab Organization for Industrialization.

title: M8 enters service
subtitle: 1940S self-propelled 75 mm howitzer of American origin
fact: The 75 mm howitzer motor carriage M8 "Scott" was a self-propelled howitzer vehicle of the United States in use during World War II.

title: M67 Zippo enters service
subtitle: Flamethrower tank
fact: The flame thrower tank M67 is an American flame tank that was briefly used by the U.S.

title: Stridsvagn m/41 enters service
subtitle: Type of medium tank
fact: Stridsvagn m/41 was a Swedish medium tank.

title: T-13 tank destroyer enters service
subtitle: Type of Tank destroyer
fact: The T-13 was a tank destroyer in use with the Belgian armed forces before World War II and during the Battle of Belgium.

title: TACAM R-2 enters service
subtitle: Type of tank destroyer
fact: The TACAM R-2 was a Romanian tank destroyer used during World War II.

title: Batignolles-Châtillon Char 25T enters service
subtitle: Prototype French medium tank of the 1950s
fact: The Batignolles-Châtillon Char 25T was an early Cold War medium tank of France, developed in 1954 by the Batignolles-Châtillon company.

title: GKN Simba enters service
subtitle: Type of armored personnel carrier
fact: The Simba is a wheeled armoured personnel carrier designed by GKN Sankey.

title: Panzer 58 enters service
subtitle: Type of medium tank
fact: The Mittlerer Panzer 1958 or Panzer 58 was a medium tank of Swiss design.

title: Loyd Carrier enters service
subtitle: Type of armored personnel carrier
fact: The Loyd Carrier was one of a number of small tracked vehicles used by the British and Commonwealth forces in the Second World War to transport equipment and men about the battlefield.

title: Bravia Chaimite enters service
subtitle: Portuguese armored vehicle
fact: The Bravia Chaimite is an armoured vehicle with all wheel drive axles built by the Portuguese company Bravia and used by the Portuguese Army in the Portuguese colonial wars in Angola, Mozambique and Portuguese Guinea, from 1967 to 1974 when it ended.

title: OT-62 TOPAS enters service
subtitle: 1960S armored personnel carrier family
fact: The OT-62 TOPAS is a series of amphibious tracked armoured personnel carriers developed jointly by the Polish People's Republic and Czechoslovakia (ČSSR).

title: Garford-Putilov armored car enters service
subtitle: Type of armored car
fact: Garford–Putilov armoured cars were armoured fighting vehicles produced in Russia during the First World War era.

title: AMR 33 enters service
subtitle: French light tank developed during the Interbellum
fact: The Automitrailleuse de Reconnaissance Renault Modèle 1933 was a French light cavalry tank developed during the Interbellum and used in the Second World War.

title: AMX-30E enters service
subtitle: Main battle tank
fact: The AMX-30E (E stands for España, Spanish for Spain) is a Spanish main battle tank based on France's AMX-30.

title: Panhard VCR enters service
subtitle: Armored personnel carrier
fact: The Panhard VCR is a light armored personnel carrier (APC) designed by Panhard for the export market and later used by several countries.

title: Type 3 Ka-Chi enters service
subtitle: Type of amphibious tank
fact: The "special amphibious tank Type 3 Ka-Chi" was an amphibious medium tank developed by the Imperial Japanese Navy in World War II.

title: BA-6 enters service
subtitle: Soviet armored car
fact: The BA-3 was a heavy armored car developed in the Soviet Union in 1933, followed by a slightly changed model BA-6 in 1936.

title: AMC 34 enters service
subtitle: Light tank
fact: The AMC 34 was a French tank originally built for the French Army's cavalry units.

title: KV-1 enters service
subtitle: Soviet heavy tank
fact: The Kliment Voroshilov tanks are a series of Soviet heavy tanks named after the Soviet defence commissar and politician Kliment Voroshilov who operated with the Red Army during World War II.

title: Type 73 Armored Personnel Carrier enters service
subtitle: Armored personnel carrier model by Mitsubishi
fact: The Type 73 armored personnel carrier is a tracked armored personnel carrier that entered service with Japan Ground Self-Defense Force in 1973.

title: Semovente 75/34 enters service
subtitle: Self-propelled gun
fact: The Semovente da 75/34 was an Italian self-propelled gun developed and used during World War II.

title: Medium Mark III enters service
subtitle: Medium tank
fact: The Medium Mark III was a medium tank developed by the United Kingdom during the Interwar period.

title: Raad-2 enters service
subtitle: Iranian self-propelled howitzer
fact: Raad-2 is an Iranian self-propelled howitzer.

title: Stormartillerivagn m/43 enters service
subtitle: Type of assault gun
fact: Stormartillerivagn m/43 was an assault gun based on Stridsvagn m/41 SII chassis, a Swedish development of a license-built Czechoslovak TNH light tank.

title: Standard Beaverette enters service
subtitle: Type of armored car
fact: Standard Car 4x2, or Car Armoured Light Standard, better known as the Beaverette, was a British improvised armoured car produced during the Second World War.

title: TACAM T-60 enters service
subtitle: Type of tank destroyer
fact: The TACAM T-60 was a Romanian tank destroyer used during World War II.

title: T-24 enters service
subtitle: Soviet medium tank
fact: The T-24 was a Soviet medium tank built in 1931.

title: 39M Csaba enters service
subtitle: Armored car
fact: The 39M Csaba (t͡ʃɒbɒ) was a Hungarian armoured car used for liaison and reconnaissance.

title: Type 4 Ka-Tsu enters service
subtitle: Type of weapon
fact: The special Type 4 Launch Ka-Tsu was a Japanese amphibious landing craft of World War II.

title: Vilkha enters service
subtitle: Self-propelled multiple rocket launcher by Luch
fact: Vilkha is a Ukrainian heavy multiple rocket launcher that fires guided missiles.

title: Bergepanzer 2 enters service
subtitle: Armoured recovery vehicle model
fact: The Bergepanzer 2 (BPz-2) is an armored recovery vehicle based on the Leopard 1 main battle tank, developed in the 1960s and manufactured by Atlas-MaK Maschinenbau for the West German army.

title: T-40 enters service
subtitle: Soviet amphibious tank
fact: The T-40 amphibious scout tank was an amphibious light tank used by the Soviet Union during World War II.

title: Kubuś enters service
subtitle: Polish World War II armored car
fact: Kubuś is a Polish improvised fighting vehicle used by the Home Army in the Warsaw Uprising during World War II.

title: Lancia IZM enters service
subtitle: Type of armored car
fact: The Lancia 1Z and the Lancia 1ZM were two variations of an Italian armoured car built during World War I and which saw limited service during that war, the interwar period, and during World War II.

title: Tančík vz. 33 enters service
subtitle: Type of tankette
fact: The Tančík vz. 33 was a Czechoslovak-designed tankette used mainly by Slovakia during World War II.

title: KMW Grizzly enters service
subtitle: Armored car
fact: The GFF4, previously KMW Grizzly, is a medium weight MRAP armored personnel carrier, developed by Krauss-Maffei Wegmann (KMW), designed for operation with the German Army based on the 6x6 Trakker chassis from Iveco adapted to meet the needs of the German Army.

title: 10TP enters service
subtitle: Medium tank from Poland
fact: The 10TP was a Polish light cruiser tank that never progressed beyond the prototype stage.

title: Type 1 Ho-Ki enters service
subtitle: Type of weapon
fact: The Type 1 Ho-Ki was a tracked armored personnel carrier (APC) developed by the Imperial Japanese Army in World War II.

title: Type 81 Surface-to-air Missile enters service
subtitle: Type of mobile, short-range surface-to-air missile
fact: The Type 81 surface-to-air missile (81式短距離地対空誘導弾) or Tan-SAM (短SAM) is a Japanese developed surface-to-air missile currently in service with the Japan Ground Self-Defense Force.

title: DAF M39 enters service
subtitle: Armoured vehicle
fact: The Pantserwagen M39 or DAF Pantrado 3 was a Dutch 6×4 armoured car produced in the late 1930s for the Royal Dutch Army.

title: Straussler V-4 enters service
subtitle: Light tank
fact: The Straussler V-4, also known as Light Tank V4 was a Hungarian amphibious light tank design of the interwar period and it was designed by Nicholas Straussler.

title: Bionix AFV enters service
subtitle: Infantry fighting vehicle
fact: The Bionix (BX) is a family of tracked Singaporean armoured fighting vehicles jointly developed by the former Defence Material Organisation of the Ministry of Defence of Singapore (now Defence Science and Technology Agency (DSTA)), and the former Singapore Technologies Automotive (STA) (now ST Engineering).

title: Austin Armored Car enters service
subtitle: Armored car
fact: The Austin armoured car was a British armoured car produced during the First World War.

title: Pansarvärnskanonvagn m/43 enters service
subtitle: Tank destroyer developed by Landsverk
fact: The Pansarvärnskanonvagn m/43 was a tank destroyer developed by Landsverk.

title: Guy armoured car enters service
subtitle: Type of weapon
fact: The Guy Armoured Car was a British armoured car produced in limited numbers during Second World War.

title: Lanchester 4x2 armoured car enters service
subtitle: British armoured car
fact: The Lanchester armoured car was a British armoured car built on the chassis of the Lanchester "Sporting Forty", it saw wide service with the Royal Naval Air Service and British Army during the First World War.

title: HWK-11 enters service
subtitle: Armored personnel carrier model by SEDENA and Henschel
fact: The Sedena Henschel HWK-11 was a joint project between the Mexican Secretariat of National Defense (SEDENA) and Henschel Wehrtechnik GmbH of West Germany.

title: Semovente 47/32 enters service
subtitle: Self-propelled gun
fact: The Semovente L40 da 47/32 was an Italian self-propelled gun built during World War II.

title: KhTZ-16 enters service
subtitle: Soviet improvised armored vehicle
fact: The KhTZ-16 was a Soviet improvised fighting vehicle of the Second World War, built on the chassis of an STZ-3 tractor.

title: FAI armored car enters service
subtitle: Soviet armored car
fact: The FAI (Ford-A Izhorskiy) armoured car was a replacement for the D-8 armoured car, used by the Soviet Union from the early 1930s to early 1940s.

title: Mungo ESK enters service
subtitle: Armored fighting vehicle
fact: The Mungo ESK is an air-transportable, armoured multirole transport vehicle operated by the Airmobile Operations Division and the Rapid Forces Division of the German Army.

title: Type 60 enters service
subtitle: 1950S self-propelled recoilless gun
fact: The Type-60 self-propelled 106 mm recoilless gun is a light anti-tank vehicle developed by Japan in the late 1950s.

title: Type 4 Ke-Nu enters service
subtitle: Light tank
fact: The Type 4 Ke-Nu was a light tank of the Imperial Japanese Army in World War II.

title: BA-11 enters service
subtitle: Type of armored car
fact: The BA-11 or Broneavtomobil 11 was an armored car developed in the Soviet Union.
